steps.text: The volume of a right cylinder is $V = \pi r^2 h$. The height is 4 inches longer than the radius, so $h = r + 4$.

• A: $4\pi r^3$ requires $h = 4r$, not $h = r + 4$. Eliminated.
  • B: $\pi(2r)^3 = 8\pi r^3$ — uses $(2r)^3$, unrelated to the height constraint. Eliminated.
  • C: $\pi r^2 + 4\pi r = \pi r(r + 4)$ — missing one factor of $r$. Eliminated.
  • D: $\pi r^3 + 4\pi r^2 = \pi r^2(r + 4)$ — matches exactly. That matches choice D — D is correct.
